commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=81bfa3a89f8541f06b7de2a03b39cf4d59f7c7de)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds two selectable GitHub pull request templates and documents `template=` compare URLs in the existing default template.

PR surface: Docs +114. Total +114 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows `full_change.md` diverges from the current PR-body contract, and patch validation reproduces trailing-whitespace failures. No OpenClaw runtime reproduction is applicable.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review remains the right lane because this changes contributor PR-authoring policy and the external proof gap cannot be repaired on the contributor's behalf.

target.title: docs: add selectable PR templates
target.body:
Closes #59737

## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw already has a default pull request template, but contributors do not have separate template choices for small fixes versus larger changes.

This adds selectable PR templates so small fixes can use a lightweight template, while larger or riskier changes can still use a fuller template.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds two GitHub pull request templates:

* `quick_fix.md` for typos, documentation fixes, small bug fixes, and low-risk maintenance changes.
* `full_change.md` for features, behavior changes, refactors, compatibility changes, or higher-risk work.

The existing default PR template remains in place, and it now documents the supported `template=` selection path for choosing either optional template.

## User Impact

First-time contributors and small-fix authors get a shorter PR form that is easier to complete.

Maintainers still get a fuller template option when a change needs more context, validation, or risk explanation.

Contributors who are unsure which template to use can continue using the default PR template.

## Evidence

Markdown/GitHub-template-only change.

Verified that the two new template files exist:

```bash
ls .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE
```

Expected files:

```text
full_change.md
quick_fix.md
```

Verified that the default PR template documents the supported `template=` selection path for both optional templates.

Template selection URLs:

Quick-fix template:

```text
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/compare/main...2001J:docs-pr-template-options-59737?quick_pull=1&template=quick_fix.md
```

Full-change template:

```text
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/compare/main...2001J:docs-pr-template-options-59737?quick_pull=1&template=full_change.md
```

I opened both URLs in GitHub’s PR creation flow and confirmed each URL loads the expected PR body template.

No runtime tests were run because this only changes GitHub PR template Markdown files.
target.author: 2001j

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
